description:
Your role: You are an on-call incident-responder who must cut an emergency patch release for the internal tooling project located at
    /home/user/incident-tools

    Current situation
    -----------------
    1. /home/user/incident-tools is an existing **git** repository cloned in the working
       directory.  All recent hot-fixes were committed directly to the *main* branch, but
       no formal release has been made since version 1.2.3.
    2. The repository already contains these two version–tracking files:
         • /home/user/incident-tools/VERSION          (plain-text, one line: the current version)
         • /home/user/incident-tools/package.json     (JSON, has a top-level field called "version")
    3. A human left the last three git commit messages after v1.2.3 as:
         a.  fix(parser): handle null bytes in log stream
         b.  chore(deps): bump lodash from 4.17.20 to 4.17.21
         c.  docs: update contact escalation path
       None of these commits introduce breaking changes or new features, therefore the next
       semantic version bump must be **patch** → 1.2.4.

    Your tasks
    ----------
    1. Calculate the new version (1.2.4) and update **both** version-tracking files so
       that they contain exactly "1.2.4".
    2. Pre-pend (i.e. insert at the very top) a new heading in
       /home/user/incident-tools/CHANGELOG.md with the following exact shape
       (respect the blank lines, hashes and bullet characters because the
       automated tests will parse them literally):

       ## [1.2.4] - YYYY-MM-DD
       ### Added
       _Nothing_

       ### Changed
       _Nothing_

       ### Fixed
       - handle null bytes in log stream

       ### Security
       _Nothing_

       (Replace `YYYY-MM-DD` with today’s date in ISO-8601, for example 2024-03-30.)

    3. Create a **signed** git tag named v1.2.4 that points to the latest commit on
       main *after* your file edits.  The tag annotation text must be the single line:
       “Release 1.2.4”.
    4. Generate a machine-readable audit artefact at
       /home/user/incident-tools/.release_logs/release_1.2.4.json
       The file **must** satisfy ALL of the following:
         • valid JSON
         • exact key order (tests check order):
             {
               "old_version": "1.2.3",
               "new_version": "1.2.4",
               "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
               "commits_included": [
                 "fix(parser): handle null bytes in log stream",
                 "chore(deps): bump lodash from 4.17.20 to 4.17.21",
                 "docs: update contact escalation path"
               ],
               "git_tag": "v1.2.4"
             }
         • same ISO-8601 date you used in CHANGELOG.md.
    5. Ensure the directory /home/user/incident-tools/.release_logs already exists
       (create it if necessary) and set its permissions so that any regular user on
       the system may *read* but only you may *write* (octal 0755 on the directory
       and 0644 on the JSON file are acceptable).

    Verification the grader will perform
    ------------------------------------
    1. It will read /home/user/incident-tools/VERSION and package.json and expect the
       string 1.2.4.
    2. It will parse the top section of CHANGELOG.md and compare it byte-for-byte
       with the pattern shown above (allowing only the correct date substitution).
    3. It will run `git tag -l --format="%(refname:short)"` and expect **v1.2.4**
       to be present, and then check the annotation body for “Release 1.2.4”.
    4. It will load the JSON log file and validate every field as well as the key
       order.
    5. Permissions of .release_logs and its children will be stat-checked.

    Hints
    -----
    • Do not delete existing sections of CHANGELOG.md; just insert the new one at the top.
    • A signed tag can be created with `git tag -s`, but GPG may not be configured in
      the execution environment; if you cannot sign, create an *annotated* tag
      (`git tag -a`) instead—the test harness treats both as “signed”.
    • You may freely use standard UNIX text utilities (`jq`, `sed`, `awk`, etc.)
      already present in the container.
truth:
<!-- Initial repository state before the agent starts -->
    <repo path="/home/user/incident-tools">
        <file name="VERSION">1.2.3
        </file>
        <file name="CHANGELOG.md">## [1.2.3] - 2023-10-02
### Added
- initial release of incident-tools

### Changed
- improved log parsing performance

### Fixed
- corrected typo in README

### Security
- none
        </file>
        <file name="package.json">{
  "name": "incident-tools",
  "version": "1.2.3",
  "description": "Internal utilities for incident responders",
  "main": "index.js",
  "license": "MIT"
}
        </file>
        <!-- git commit history -->
        <git>
            <branch name="main">
                <commit id="C0" message="chore: initial 1.2.3 release"/>
                <commit id="C1" message="fix(parser): handle null bytes in log stream"/>
                <commit id="C2" message="chore(deps): bump lodash from 4.17.20 to 4.17.21"/>
                <commit id="C3" message="docs: update contact escalation path"/>
                <!-- HEAD points at C3 -->
            </branch>
            <!-- Tag v1.2.3 already exists on commit C0 -->
            <tag name="v1.2.3" commit="C0" annotation="Release 1.2.3"/>
        </git>
    </repo>

    <!-- Final state expected after agent finishes -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/incident-tools/VERSION">1.2.4
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/incident-tools/package.json">{
  "name": "incident-tools",
  "version": "1.2.4",
  "description": "Internal utilities for incident responders",
  "main": "index.js",
  "license": "MIT"
}
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/incident-tools/CHANGELOG.md" contains="## [1.2.4]"/>
        <file path="/home/user/incident-tools/.release_logs/release_1.2.4.json">{
  "old_version": "1.2.3",
  "new_version": "1.2.4",
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "commits_included": [
    "fix(parser): handle null bytes in log stream",
    "chore(deps): bump lodash from 4.17.20 to 4.17.21",
    "docs: update contact escalation path"
  ],
  "git_tag": "v1.2.4"
}
        </file>
        <git>
            <tag name="v1.2.4" annotation="Release 1.2.4"/>
        </git>
    </expected>
